sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a serpent, so called from its hissing (see the root) Gen 3:1, seq.; Exo 4:3 7:15 2Ki 18:4. Used of the constellation of the serpent or dragon in the northern part of the sky, Arab. حَيَّة Job 26:13.

(2) [Nahash], pr.n.-(a) of a town otherwise unknown, 1Ch 4:12.-(b) of a king of the Ammonites, 1Sa 11:1 2Sa 10:2, and of various men.-(c) 2Sa 17:27.-(d) 2Sa 17:25.
